item.title: 13 Dhaka hospitals shut for anomalies in 3 days
item.description: The Directorate General of Health Services shut down 13 private hospitals, clinics, and blood banks during a three-day drive in the capital after inspecting 16 healthcare facilities....

item.title: Unattended level crossings risk lives
item.description: The Bangladesh Railway has been reeling from unmanned level crossings across the country for years....

item.title: Fuel hoarding rampant
item.description: The government on Friday formed vigilance teams for 64 districts amid complaints of hoarding of and persisting queues for fuel at filling stations....

item.title: Imagining a world without Israel
item.description: I HAVE often wondered how different our world might have been had the State of Israel never come into existence. Its birth was not inevitable; it was the product of apoliticaldecision made far from Palestine. The infamous Balfour Declaration of......Politics

item.title: RETHINKING ISLAM: A woman’s reflection
item.description: I WRITE this not as a scholar, nor as a spokesperson for any institution, but as a woman shaped by a Muslim cultural world — and unsettled by what that world has become....

item.title: Untapped natural resources
item.description: BANGLADESH is recognised for its strong economic growth, increased exports and progress in reducing poverty. However, the country has several valuable natural resources that remain only partially explored or not used efficiently. These include natural......

item.title: Road we keep dying on
item.description: ON MARCH 25, a bus carrying people home after Eid plunged nine metres into the River Padma at the Daulatdia ferry terminal in Rajbari. Twenty-six people died — seven children, 11 women and eight men — many of them trapped inside the......

item.title: Ferry terminal disaster rooted in negligence
item.description: THE train of death that began with Eid-time travel has grown longer with the death of 26 more people after a bus waiting on the pontoon at the Daulatdia terminal in Rajbari fell into the River Padma at a quarter past 5:00pm on March 25. The bus, reportedly......

item.title: Ordinance debate falls short of political sagacity
item.description: WHILE a special parliamentary committee reviews 133 ordinances promulgated by the interim government’s tenure under Article 93(1) of the constitution, with its report due by April 2, the tenor of its first two days of discussions, as the media......

item.title: Syria’s Druze violence may be war crimes: UN
item.description: War crimes and crimes against humanity might have been committed when brutal violence tore through Syria’s southern Sweida province last year, killing more than 1,700, UN investigators said Friday....

item.title: Philippines, France pact deepens military cooperation
item.description: The Philippines and France have signed an agreement opening the door for their soldiers to participate in joint exercises on each other’s soil , Manila said Friday, its first such pact with a European country....

item.title: Epstein survivors sue US govt over revealed identities
item.description: Survivors of late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein sued the US government and Google on Thursday over victims’ identities being mistakenly revealed in a trove of documents published online by the justice department....

item.title: US accuses China of detaining Panama-flagged ships
item.description: The United States has accused China of detaining Panama-flagged ships after a takeover by Panamanian authorities of two ports previously controlled by a Chinese company....

item.title: Norway to hike defence budget by $11b over 10 years
item.description: Norway, a NATO member bordering Russia, said Friday it was increasing its defence budget by $11 billion over the next decade, citing a deteriorating security situation....

item.title: UK parents told to limit under-5s screen time
item.description: The United Kingdom government on Friday published its first national guidance on screen time for children, advising parents to allow no more than an hour a day for under-fives....

item.title: Lebanon at risk of humanitarian catastrophe: UN
item.description: Nearly a month into the Middle East war, Lebanon is facing a deepening humanitarian crisis that now risks teetering over into a catastrophe, the United Nations refugee agency warned Friday....

item.title: 2 more suspected shooters arrested while attempting to flee country
item.description: Two more men suspected to have shot dead Dhaka Juba Dal leader Golam Kibria were arrested early Friday as they were attempting to flee the country....Maps

item.title: BNP, Jamaat activists scuffle as Zia’s name not mentioned
item.description: Leaders and activists of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party and the Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami scuffled at Natudah union of Damurhuda upazila in Chuadanga on Thursday after the name of former president Ziaur Rahman, also the founder of the BNP, was reportedly not mentioned during an Independence Day programme....

item.title: IU mega project delayed again despite three extensions
item.description: A Tk 537.07 crore mega infrastructure development project of Islamic University in Kushtia is unlikely to be completed within the latest deadline despite three extensions, prompting the authorities to seek more time....

item.title: Bus catches fire in Jashore
item.description: A bus of Narail Paribahan caught fire due to an engine fault on Friday while travelling from Jashore to Dhaka with around 50 passengers on board....

item.title: Newborn’s body found in box at Jurain graveyard
item.description: The police have recovered the body of a one-day-old baby boy from inside a cardboard box at Jurain graveyard in the capital....Bangladesh news updates

item.title: Top bureaucracy reshuffled, eight made secretary
item.description: At least eight additional secretaries have been promoted to the rank of secretary while a retired secretary has been appointed on one-year contract as part of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government’s fresh reshuffle in the civil bureaucracy....

item.title: Jamaat leader held over attack on Palashbari police
item.description: The police on Friday arrested a local leader of Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami in connection with an alleged attack on the Palashbari police officer-in-charge and his colleagues inside the police station in Gaibandha....

item.title: Child tourist drowns in Jaflong River
item.description: A child tourist drowned in the Piyain River at Jaflong of Goainghat upazila in Sylhet on Friday morning....

item.title: Kushtia BNP leader’s remarks on DC transfer draw criticism
item.description: REMARKS by Syed Mehedi Ahmed Rumi, a defeated Bangladesh Nationalist Party candidate for the Kushtia-4 constituency in 13th Jatiya Sangsad elections, have sparked criticism after he claimed that a former deputy commissioner of the district had been transferred at his party’s initiative....

item.title: China blasts ‘false’ report on chip sales to Iran
item.description: China’s foreign ministry accused the media of publishing ‘false information’ on Friday following a report that said the country’s top semiconductor firm has sent chipmaking tools to Iran....

item.title: Arctic sea ice at lowest level ever
item.description: Arctic sea ice reached its lowest level ever recorded, statistically tying last year’s record, a leading US climate observatory for this geopolitically significant region said on Thursday....

item.title: Ukraine, Saudi sign air defence deal
item.description: Ukraine and Saudi Arabia have signed an air defence agreement during president Volodymyr Zelensky’s visit to the kingdom facing Iranian drone attacks, two senior officials told AFP on Friday....

item.title: Austria school headscarf ban sparks anger
item.description: A headscarf ban for girls under 14 in Austrian schools has stirred unease, anger and fears of further targeting of Muslims in the EU member state....

item.title: Israeli military kills two in Jerusalem raid
item.description: Israeli forces killed two Palestinian men on Friday during a raid north of Jerusalem, the Ramallah-based health ministry in the occupied West Bank and relatives said....

item.title: Cyclone triggers outages at major Australian LNG plants
item.description: Some of the world’s largest LNG plants were forced shut on Friday by a cyclone off Western Australia, squeezing fuel supplies already stretched thin by war in the Middle East....

item.title: Coast Guard opens 6 ships to public
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard has opened six ships to the public on the occasion of Independence and National Day 2026....Bangladesh news updates

item.title: Cultural organisations observe Independence Day
item.description: Various cultural organisations across Bangladesh organised special programmes focusing on the 56th anniversary of independence, which was observed on Thursday....

item.title: SUJS distributes Eid gifts among children with disabilities
item.description: The Swa-Udyogi Juba Sangho has distributed Eid gifts among children with disabilities at the Angel Care Special School at Mirpur 2 in Dhaka....

item.title: World Theatre Day observed
item.description: Bangladesh Shilpakala Academy, the Bangladesh Centre of the International Theatre Institute, and the Bangladesh Group Theatre Federation jointly observed World Theatre Day 2026 at the National Theatre Hall of BSA on Friday....

item.title: Youth hacked while undergoing treatment at hospital
item.description: A youth was hacked with sharp weapons by a group of five to six miscreants while undergoing treatment at the Kotalipara Upazila Health Complex at Kotalipara in Gopalganj on Friday morning....

item.title: Myanmar military signals leadership changes
item.description: Myanmar’s junta signalled changes in the military’s leadership ahead of the country’s annual show of force on Friday, potentially clearing the way for defence chief Min Aung Hlaing to become president....Maps

item.title: Iran warns civilians, Trump says talks ‘going well’
item.description: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards warned civilians across the Middle East Friday to stay away from areas near US forces, in a mark of defiance after president Donald Trump claimed talks to end the month-long war were ‘going well’....
